perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party will treat Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Information that is withi n t he Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, as confidential an d pr oprietary and will use such information only for the Purpose. Receiving Party will not disclos e s uch information to any third party for the duration of the Confidentiality Period without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Proprietary Informati on pr ovided to Alliance hereunder is subject to inspection by DOE or its designee upon reasonabl e not ice. Proprietary Information provided to DOE employees is protected against further disclosur e under 18 U .S.C. § 1905.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party will treat Proprietary Data and NREL Protected Information that is withi n t he Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, as confidential an d pr oprietary and will use such information only for the Purpose. Receiving Party will not disclos e s uch information to any third party for the duration of the Confidentiality Period without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Proprietary Informati on pr ovided to Alliance hereunder is subject to inspection by DOE or its designee upon reasonabl e not ice. Proprietary Information provided to DOE employees is protected against further disclosur e under 18 U .S.C. § 1905.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Proprietary Information” to “Proprietary Data” in the first sentence, while keeping it as “Proprietary Information” later in the paragraph creates inconsistency. It is unclear if both terms are reffering to the same thing. There is also an intra-textual contradiction on whether the document is refering to 'Proprietary Data' or 'Proprietary Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party will treat Proprietary Data and NREL Protected Information that is withi n t he Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, as confidential an d pr oprietary and will use such information only for the Purpose. Receiving Party will not disclos e s uch information to any third party for the duration of the Confidentiality Period without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Proprietary Informati on pr ovided to Alliance hereunder is subject to inspection by DOE or its designee upon reasonabl e not ice. Proprietary Information provided to DOE employees is protected against further disclosur e under 18 U .S.C. § 1905.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Proprietary Data' in the first sentence while the paragraph later still refers to 'Proprietary Information'. Since the definition of Misaligned Terminology states that in-text contradictions occur when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, and the explanation states that it is unclear if both terms are referring to the same thing, it creates a strong contradiction, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. Therefore, the change creates a contradiction. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party will provide access to Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Informati on t hat is within the Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, only t o R eceiving Party's employees, agents, and independent contractors who are required to have access specifically related to the Purpose, and, with respect to Alliance, to DOE or its designe e f or auditing and inspection purposes only. Receiving Party will inform individuals having access t o s uch information of the confidential nature of this information and the restrictions on its publication , disclosure, and use, and will require that such employees, agents, and independent contractors are bound by confidentiality obligations no less stringent than those stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party will provide access to Secret Information and NREL Protected Informati on t hat is within the Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, only t o R eceiving Party's employees, agents, and independent contractors who are required to have access specifically related to the Purpose, and, with respect to Alliance, to DOE or its designe e f or auditing and inspection purposes only. Receiving Party will inform individuals having access t o s uch information of the confidential nature of this information and the restrictions on its publication , disclosure, and use, and will require that such employees, agents, and independent contractors are bound by confidentiality obligations no less stringent than those stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing “Proprietary Information” with “Secret Information” introduces a new term without definition. Since the document never establishes whether “Secret Information” is the same as “Proprietary Information” or NREL Protected Information”, it is unclear which type of information is being reffered to. Since also “Proprietary Information” and “NREL Protected Information”, are being used in other sections of the document, there is an intra-textual contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure of Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information to Receiving Party does not constitute any grant, option, or license under any patent or other right now or hereinafter held by Disclosing Party or DOE. No license —express or implied— in the Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information or other proprietary right is granted hereunder other than to use the information in the manner and the extent authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Proprietary Information" with "Secret Information". The explanation states that this introduces a new term without definition, and that the document doesn't establish whether “Secret Information” is the same as “Proprietary Information” or “NREL Protected Information”. The contradicted text refers to "Proprietary Information" and/or "NREL Protected Information". Since "Secret Information" is not defined, it is unclear whether the obligations in the contradicted text apply to "Secret Information". This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text is referencing something that is no longer defined to be exactly the same. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Disclosure of Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information to Receiving Party does not constitute any grant, option, or license under any patent or other right now or hereinafter held by Disclosing Party or DOE. No license —express or implied— in the Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information or other proprietary right is granted hereunder other than to use the information in the manner and the extent authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosure of Proprietary Materials and/or NREL Protected Information to Receiving Party does not constitute any grant, option, or license under any patent or other right now or hereinafter held by Disclosing Party or DOE. No license —express or implied— in the Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information or other proprietary right is granted hereunder other than to use the information in the manner and the extent authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing “Proprietary Information” with “Proprietary Materials” in the beginning of the sentence introduces a new term without definition, that contradicts the reference of the term 'Proprietary Information' later in the text. Since also the term 'Proprietary Information' is being used in other sections of the document, there is an intra-textual contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure of Proprietary Materials and/or NREL Protected Information to Receiving Party does not constitute any grant, option, or license under any patent or other right now or hereinafter held by Disclosing Party or DOE. No license —express or implied— in the Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information or other proprietary right is granted hereunder other than to use the information in the manner and the extent authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Proprietary Materials' in the beginning of the sentence. The contradiction arises because the sentence later refers to 'Proprietary Information,' and this term is also used in the contradicted text. This introduces misaligned terminology as 'Proprietary Materials' is not defined, leading to confusion about whether it is interchangeable with 'Proprietary Information,' which is defined elsewhere, thereby creating a contradiction based on the provided Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: A Party receiving Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information will adhere to U.S. Export Administration Laws and Regulations and will not export or re- export any such Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information, any technical data, items, or products arising from such information to any country or person unless properly authorized by the U.S. Government.
perturbation.changed_text: A Party receiving Confidential Information and/or NREL Protected Information will adhere to U.S. Export Administration Laws and Regulations and will not export or re- export any such Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information, any technical data, items, or products arising from such information to any country or person unless properly authorized by the U.S. Government.
perturbation.explanation: The introduction of 'Confidential Information' at the beginning of the paragraph, while continuing to use 'Proprietary Information' later creates confusion regarding whether both terms are the same, different, or overlapping categories. This leads to in-text contradiction on what type of information the article is refering to. Since also the term 'Proprietary Information' is being used in other sections of the document, there is an intra-textual contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A Party receiving Confidential Information and/or NREL Protected Information will adhere to U.S. Export Administration Laws and Regulations and will not export or re- export any such Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information, any technical data, items, or products arising from such information to any country or person unless properly authorized by the U.S. Government.
perturbation.justification: The explanation states that the introduction of 'Confidential Information' at the beginning of the paragraph, while continuing to use 'Proprietary Information' later, creates confusion about whether the terms are the same, different, or overlapping. This confusion leads to an in-text contradiction on what type of information the article refers to. The changed text modifies the original text by adding 'Confidential Information' alongside 'Proprietary Information' and 'NREL Protected Information.' The continued use of 'Proprietary Information' alongside 'Confidential Information' in both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text results in misaligned terminology and a lack of clarity regarding which type of information is subject to the U.S. Export Administration Laws, creating ambiguity and therefore a strong contradiction, in accordance with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties and it supersedes all prior or contemporaneous communications, agreements, or understandings between the Parties concerning receipt of Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties and it supersedes all prior or contemporaneous communications, agreements, or understandings between the Parties concerning receipt of Private Information and/or NREL Protected Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Proprietary Information' to 'Private Information' introduces a new undefined term, creating confusion as to what the agreement covers. There is also intra-textual contradiction on what the document is refering to. Since also the term 'Proprietary Information' is being used in other sections of the document, there is an intra-textual contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein, “Proprietary Information” means information that (i) embodies trade secrets as defined under 18 U.S.C. § 1839 or (ii) is commercial or financial information that is privileged or confidential under the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(4)), and that is developed at private expense outside this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Proprietary Information' with 'Private Information'. The Contradicted Text defines 'Proprietary Information'. By introducing 'Private Information' and using it where 'Proprietary Information' was, the contract now refers to an undefined term, creating intra-textual contradiction and uncertainty about the subject matter of the agreement. Since the Contradicted Text provides a definition of 'Proprietary Information' the change breaks consistency and introduces ambiguity. According to the Perturbation Explanation this is an intra-textual contradiction.
